notes:
Selection now paints the way Dropbox Paper does, the caret stops fighting the bullet gutter, and one more sync edge case around deleting a parent block is closed.

**Fixes & polish**

- **Text selection hugs each line, like Dropbox Paper.** The highlight now follows your text on both sides instead of running to a straight left edge — it no longer paints the empty space to the left of nested bullets, and bullet markers and to-do checkboxes stay *outside* the highlight on every row. A selection spanning several indent levels steps in with the text, the way Paper draws it. (PRD Addendum 23 §V2 "Option B", decided by Soham 2026-07-20.)
- **Cmd+Left / Home lands at the start of the text.** Jumping to the start of a line now stops at the first character instead of slipping into the bullet's marker gutter, and **Shift+Home / Shift+Cmd+Left** selects from the caret to that line start. (PRD Addendum 23 §V2; the caret line-start clamp.)
- **Deleting a note's parent block keeps its children.** When another device deletes a block that had children under it, those children are now *outdented* into place instead of vanishing along with their parent. (PR #571; Sync Deep Audit 2026-07-17 follow-up.)

**Under the hood**

- Selection-band geometry is now **per-row text-hugging**: the shared `commonLeft` edge is gone, every row hugs its own selected-text extent on both sides, and non-selectable leading chrome (the to-do checkbox `<label>`) is dropped from a row's text extent so the band never bleeds left of the text. (PRD Addendum 23 §V2.1′–V2.6′; ADR-0015.)
- Block-delete sync **apply promotes a deleted block's children** (re-parenting them to the block's parent) before the delete lands, so a cascade delete can no longer discard live descendants on the receiving device. (PR #571; Sync Deep Audit 2026-07-17 follow-up.)
